name: Loanstar Title Loans
description_short: LoanStar Title Loans provides fast cash advances up to $10,000 by using your vehicle title as collateral. Founded in 1990, they operate multiple locations across the San Antonio area.
description_long:
LoanStar Title Loans was founded in 1990 in Jonesboro, Georgia, and has operated for over 25 years as a vehicle title lending company. They position themselves as helping hardworking Americans access cash by leveraging the equity in their vehicles. The company operates multiple physical locations throughout the San Antonio, Texas area, including the primary location at 1801 SW Military Drive and four additional nearby branches.

LoanStar's core service is auto title loans, which allow borrowers to receive cash advances up to $10,000 by using their car title as collateral. The application process is streamlined, requiring only a vehicle, photo ID, and a clear vehicle title. They accept multiple payment methods including cash, check, debit card, and Western Union transfers. The company offers both multiple installment and single installment loan options, with published fee schedules available for transparency on costs for loan amounts of $600, $1,200, and $2,000.

LoanStar distinguishes itself through its long operational history and multiple convenient locations within the San Antonio metro area. All their locations maintain consistent operating hours (Monday-Friday 10:00 AM–6:00 PM, Saturday 9:00 AM–2:00 PM), making access predictable for customers. They provide fee schedules upfront and display licensing information and OCCC (Office of Consumer Credit Commissioner) notices on their website, suggesting regulatory compliance. The company also offers online account management features including login, registration, and online payment options.

Title loans are a high-cost emergency borrowing option and should only be used when other alternatives are unavailable. While LoanStar provides quick access to cash for those with vehicles, borrowers risk losing their vehicle if they cannot repay. The specific APR, finance charges, and repayment terms are not disclosed on the main website pages reviewed, requiring customers to review individual loan disclosures or contact the company directly. This is a legitimate short-term cash solution for vehicle owners facing genuine emergencies, but the cost structure and collateral risk warrant careful consideration.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Loans up to $10,000 available, higher than most payday alternative lenders
Multiple convenient San Antonio-area locations with consistent weekday and Saturday hours
Fast cash access described as a core benefit, typical of emergency title loans
Accepts multiple payment methods (cash, check, debit card, Western Union) for loan repayment
Published fee schedules for $600, $1,200, and $2,000 loans providing upfront cost transparency
Both single and multiple installment options available to match different repayment preferences
Online account management features including login, registration, and online payment capability
Licensed and regulated with OCCC notices displayed, indicating compliance oversight
cons: Vehicle title used as collateral—borrowers risk losing their car if unable to repay
APR and total finance charges not disclosed on main website; requires requesting individual loan disclosures
Title loans are inherently high-cost; no mention of APR caps or comparison to payday-alternatives category options
Limited information about loan terms, repayment periods, or rollover policies on the reviewed pages
No alternative products offered for those who may not own a vehicle or prefer unsecured borrowing
best_for: Vehicle owners facing genuine emergencies who need $1,000–$10,000 quickly and have no other credit options
Borrowers with poor credit who cannot qualify for personal loans or credit-based financing
San Antonio residents who value in-person service and same-day approval at a physical location
diagnosis: LoanStar Title Loans is best for vehicle owners in the San Antonio area who need emergency cash quickly and have exhausted other borrowing options. The primary caveat is that title loans carry high costs and collateral risk—borrowers must be confident they can repay to avoid losing their vehicle. Compare rates and terms with payday-alternative lenders (credit union PALs, CDFIs) before committing, as those options may offer lower APRs if you qualify.
services: Auto title loans (car title loans) up to $10,000
Online loan registration and account creation
In-person loan applications at physical storefronts
Payment acceptance via cash, check, debit card, and Western Union
Fee disclosures for $600, $1,200, and $2,000 loan amounts
Directions and location information for five San Antonio-area branches
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
address: 1801 SW Military Dr, San Antonio, TX 78221
